title: rosa alpina var. hispida
intro: Rosa alpina var. hispida, also known as the alpine rose, is a fragrant, pink or white-flowering perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. The medicinal properties of Rosa alpina var. hispida have been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
